name: Meat Loaves Italianos
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Quaker Oats, uncooked (quick or old-fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a large bowl, mix together oats, 3/4 cup tomato sauce, 3/4 cup olives, egg (if using), 1 teaspoon onion, Italian seasoning, and salt.
3. Add ground beef and mix lightly but thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined.
4. Shape the mixture into four 4x2-inch loaves and place them in an 8-inch square glass baking dish or 9-inch glass pie plate.
5. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through. Drain any excess fat if needed.
6. In a separate bowl, mix together remaining tomato sauce, olives, onion, garlic powder, and (optionally) salt.
7. Cook this mixture over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes, or until hot.
8. Spoon the sauce over the meat loaves and sprinkle with cheese.
9. Bake for an additional 2 minutes, or until the cheese is melted.
MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS:
1. Prepare the meat mixture as directed above and shape into four 4x2-inch loaves in a dish that fits in the microwave.
2. Cover the dish with wax paper.
3. Microwave on HIGH for 5 to 7 minutes, or until the meat is no longer pink and has reached an internal temperature of 160°F, rotating the dish halfway through cooking time.
4. Allow the loaves to cool slightly before serving.
5. In a separate container, mix together remaining tomato sauce, olives, onion, garlic powder, and (optionally) salt in a microwave-safe measuring cup.
6. Microwave for an additional 1-1/2 to 2-1/2 minutes, or until hot.
7. Spoon the sauce over the cooled meat loaves and sprinkle with cheese.
8. Return the dish to the microwave and cook on HIGH for 1 minute more, or until the cheese is melted.
